quote.lead: Built a Shorts pipeline that turned a tiny audience
quote.punch: into a 7-figure coaching engine in 90 days

hero.description: How we packaged a stalled coaching brand into a Shorts engine that paid for itself in the first 30 days.

challenges.title: A great offer, no distribution
challenges.summary: Spencer had a proven coaching curriculum and a tiny, hard-won audience — but no system to turn either into reach.

items.title: Stuck at 5K subscribers for two years
items.description: Long-form uploads were inconsistent and the channel never broke past a loyal core that already paid.

items.title: Every Reel started from scratch
items.description: No hook library, no template, no review loop — each post was a one-off lottery ticket.

items.title: Ad spend was the only growth lever
items.description: Paid acquisition was carrying the entire pipeline, and the CAC kept climbing month-over-month.

solutions.title: A Shorts pipeline with a daily heartbeat
solutions.summary: We replaced ad-hoc content with a repeatable pipeline — hooks, edits, captions, and review running on the same clock every week.

items.title: Hook-first scripting framework
items.description: Twelve proven opening patterns, mapped to coaching pain points, that the editing team could pull from on day one.

items.title: Five-Reel-a-week production rhythm
items.description: A two-editor rotation with structured review windows, so cadence never slipped — even on launch weeks.

items.title: Lead-capture funnel under every post
items.description: Every Reel ended on a clear next step — a free workshop opt-in that fed the coaching offer directly.

growth.title: From break-even to a 7-figure coaching engine
growth.summary: Inside 90 days the channel went from a side project to the single largest source of qualified leads in the business.

cta.description: Tell us the offer, the channel, and the bottleneck. We will show you exactly how we would build it.

quote.lead: Replaced paid social with a relentless organic Reel cadence

hero.description: How a fast-growing apparel brand replaced a paid-heavy funnel with an organic Reels engine — without losing a single conversion.

challenges.title: Margins disappearing into the ad account
challenges.summary: Northwind had product-market fit, but every new dollar of revenue was being spent on Meta — and the iOS update was making it worse.

items.title: Rising CAC eating the margin
items.description: Cost per acquired customer had climbed nearly 60% in twelve months, and the team was running out of room to absorb it.

items.title: Organic channels barely existed
items.description: The Instagram grid was lifestyle photos, nothing was happening on Reels, and TikTok was empty.

items.title: No system for repurposing UGC
items.description: Plenty of creator footage was coming in — it was sitting in folders no one was editing into anything.

solutions.title: An organic-first content stack
solutions.summary: We built a UGC-driven Reel system that funneled into product pages, and used the paid budget to amplify only what worked organically.

items.title: UGC editing playbook
items.description: A standard treatment for every creator drop — hook, demo, social proof, CTA — so each clip became three Reels.

items.title: Daily Reel cadence across two channels
items.description: IG and TikTok publishing 1+ Reels per day, each with a platform-specific re-edit, captions, and trend audio.

items.title: Whitelist amplification instead of cold ads
items.description: Only Reels that broke the organic threshold were boosted, so ad spend rode the back of free reach.

growth.title: Same revenue, half the spend
growth.summary: Six months in, organic was the dominant source of new customers and the ad account was a multiplier — not the engine.

cta.description: Let us audit your funnel and show you what an organic-first content engine would look like for your brand.

quote.punch: built a community around a brand-new dev tool

hero.description: How a new developer tool turned founder-led video into the single highest-converting top-of-funnel channel in the business.

challenges.title: Great product, invisible launch
challenges.summary: Linear Studio shipped, did well on Product Hunt, then went quiet — there was no system to turn weekly product progress into ongoing reach.

items.title: Founder camera-shy on social
items.description: Brilliant on a podcast, frozen on Reels — there was no structure that made on-camera predictable.

items.title: Product updates buried in changelogs
items.description: Every shipped feature was a story no one outside the team ever heard, because nobody was editing them into videos.

items.title: Demos were one-offs, never repurposed
items.description: Sales demos lived inside Loom, never made it to YouTube, and certainly never became Shorts.

solutions.title: A founder-led short-form engine
solutions.summary: We built a calm, predictable production loop that turned the founder's weekly product work into daily content — without burning him out.

items.title: One-hour-a-week founder shoot
items.description: A tight scripted block once a week that produced 5+ Shorts and a flagship long-form drop.

items.title: Demo-to-short pipeline
items.description: Every sales demo recorded, mined for the best 30 seconds, and re-edited as standalone Shorts.

items.title: Community-led feedback loop
items.description: We surfaced top comments back into the next week's scripts, so the audience felt heard inside the content itself.

growth.title: An audience that compounds into pipeline
growth.summary: Demo bookings, free-trial signups, and warm inbound now ride on top of a self-reinforcing video flywheel.

cta.description: We will run a free audit on your founder content and map out exactly what the next 90 days could look like.

quote.lead: Restructured the format and doubled the cadence,
quote.punch: turning a vlog channel into a 7-figure brand

hero.description: How a creator-led channel doubled cadence, restructured every video around retention, and turned an audience into a real business.

challenges.title: Loyal audience, flat revenue
challenges.summary: Nick had a die-hard fanbase but the channel had plateaued — uploads were inconsistent and monetization barely moved with view count.

items.title: Watch time collapsing after the intro
items.description: The first 60 seconds were doing all the work, and retention was falling off a cliff before the value hit.

items.title: One video every two weeks
items.description: Cadence kept slipping because the workflow was held together by a single editor and a single overworked founder.

items.title: No funnel beyond AdSense
items.description: Every view monetized at the platform rate — there was no owned product capturing the most engaged viewers.

solutions.title: Format-led, weekly, monetized
solutions.summary: We rebuilt the channel around a retention-first format and connected every video to an owned-funnel monetization layer.

items.title: Retention-first format rewrite
items.description: Hook, payoff loop, mid-roll re-hook, satisfying close — applied to every long-form upload.

items.title: Weekly upload cadence, twice over
items.description: Two editors in rotation plus a thumbnail specialist meant Nick never edited again — he just shot and reviewed.

items.title: Owned-product funnel below every video
items.description: A creator product positioned in the first comment and pinned link, fed by a custom landing experience.

growth.title: A real business behind the channel
growth.summary: Watch time quadrupled, ARR crossed seven figures, and the channel stopped depending on AdSense to be a viable business.

cta.description: Tell us the channel and the offer — we will map out exactly what to change, in what order, to ship a creator business behind it.

quote.lead: Designed a studio workflow that lets a 4-person team
quote.punch: ship 100+ Reels every month, on brand

hero.description: How a four-person editing studio went from 30 deliverables a month to 100+ on-brand Reels — without hiring a single new editor.

challenges.title: Volume capped by chaos, not capacity
challenges.summary: Pillar's editors were good, but the studio was held together by Slack and willpower — every new client felt like an existential threat.

items.title: No shared editing standards
items.description: Every editor delivered slightly differently, and revisions multiplied because there was nothing to point a junior editor at.

items.title: Review feedback scattered across tools
items.description: Comments lived in Loom, in Slack, in Drive, in emails — nothing tracked which round of revision a project was actually on.

items.title: Project ingest by memory
items.description: There was no system for tagging selects, b-roll, or graphics — every project re-built the same assets from scratch.

solutions.title: A studio OS in 30 days
solutions.summary: We rebuilt Pillar's pipeline around a single source of truth for projects, review, and assets — then trained the team to lean on it.

items.title: Editing standards manual
items.description: Brand kits, captions, sound design, and motion baselines documented so any editor could pick up any project.

items.title: Frame.io-first review loop
items.description: All client feedback funneled into a single tool with clear revision rounds — endless revision spirals went away.

items.title: Tagged, searchable asset library
items.description: Selects, b-roll, and graphics indexed across projects so the studio compounded its own footage instead of starting cold.

growth.title: Triple the output, same four editors
growth.summary: The studio ships 3x the volume with the same headcount, the on-call founder no longer touches the timeline, and clients keep their retainer.

cta.description: Send us your current workflow and we will tell you the three changes that would unlock the next 3x of volume — without hiring.

quote.lead: Repositioned the hook formula and launched a Shorts-first calendar

hero.description: How a fitness brand repositioned its hook formula and launched a Shorts-first calendar that compounded month over month.

challenges.title: Authority, no reach
challenges.summary: Inside Out had elite practitioners and a small, devoted following — but their hooks were textbook and their reach had been flat for a year.

items.title: Hooks led with credentials, not pain
items.description: Every video opened with what the brand wanted to say, not what the viewer was actually scrolling past.

items.title: No publishing calendar
items.description: Posts went up when someone remembered — there was no consistent surface for the algorithm to learn from.

items.title: Lead capture was an afterthought
items.description: Even when a video popped, the funnel underneath it converted at almost nothing.

solutions.title: A Shorts-first content engine
solutions.summary: We rewrote the hook formula around viewer pain, locked the calendar to a predictable rhythm, and built a real lead-capture layer beneath it.

items.title: Pain-led hook formula
items.description: Eight repeatable opening patterns, each tied to a specific health pain point, that anyone on the team could write to.

items.title: Locked Shorts-first calendar
items.description: Daily Shorts, a weekly long-form deep dive, and a fixed publish window — so the algorithm could finally learn the channel.

items.title: Lead-magnet ladder below every post
items.description: Every Short pointed to a tight free resource that fed into the paid offer — measured weekly, tuned monthly.

growth.title: Reach that compounds, leads that convert
growth.summary: Monthly views crossed 500K inside two quarters, the lead pipeline grew nearly 10x, and the team finally had a content engine they could plan around.

cta.description: Send over your last 30 days of content — we will pull the hooks apart and show you exactly what to change first.
